chapter: Chapter: Pressure, Winds, Storms and Cyclones

questions.question: What is pressure?
questions.answer: Pressure is the force acting on a unit area.

questions.question: Write the formula for pressure.
What is the SI unit of pressure?
What is atmospheric pressure?
questions.answer: Atmospheric pressure is the pressure exerted by the air around us.

questions.question: What causes atmospheric pressure?
questions.answer: It is caused by the weight of air in the atmosphere.

questions.question: What is wind?
questions.answer: Wind is the movement of air.

questions.question: Air moves from which region to which region?
questions.answer: From high-pressure regions to low-pressure regions.

questions.question: What instrument measures atmospheric pressure?
What is a storm?
questions.answer: A storm is a disturbance in the atmosphere with strong winds and rain.

questions.question: What is a cyclone?
questions.answer: A cyclone is a large storm with strong rotating winds.

questions.question: Where do most cyclones form?
What is the calm center of a cyclone called?
Why do camels have broad feet?
Why do sharp knives cut easily?
questions.answer: Because they produce greater pressure on a small area.

questions.question: What happens to pressure when area increases?
What happens to pressure when force increases?
What type of air rises when heated?
What happens when warm air rises?
What fills the space when warm air rises?
questions.answer: Cool air moves in to fill the space.

questions.question: Name a natural disaster caused by strong winds.
What is the main energy source for cyclones?
Why are tractors fitted with wide tyres?
questions.answer: To reduce pressure on the ground.

questions.question: What does a barometer measure?
Why do mountaineers find it difficult to breathe at high altitudes?
questions.answer: Because atmospheric pressure decreases with height.

questions.question: What helps scientists predict storms?
